label: agent loop • cancellation after the first tool call exits cleanly with no later writes
query: Do these steps in order, one tool call at a time: (1) read step1.txt, (2) create done-a.txt containing A, (3) create done-b.txt containing B, (4) create done-c.txt containing C. Do them strictly one per turn.
notes: Interruption non-negotiable: cancelAfterToolCalls flips the loop's isCancelled hook true once ONE tool call has been processed, so the run must exit 'cancelled' cleanly (allowedExits) and — critically — none of the later writes may land. The file assertions are the teeth: whatever the first processed call was, done-c.txt must never exist because the run was cancelled before reaching it. Off-CI (needs a model).
